title: Stelis flaccida
taxonomy: Stelis flaccida is classified within the family Orchidaceae and the genus Stelis. The species Stelis flaccida is native to Central and South America. Within the Orchidaceae family, Stelis flaccida is a part of the subtribe Pleurothallidinae. The subtribe Pleurothallidinae is further divided into several genera, with Stelis being one of the largest. Stelis flaccida's classification is significant for understanding the diversity of orchid species in the region.

intro: Stelis flaccida, also known as Stelis, is a flowering orchid species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can help to alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues and skin infections. The bioactive constituents of Stelis flaccida include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Stelis include teas and infusions, as well as extracts and capsules, which are used to support immune function and promote overall well-being.
